summary.intent: Display the actual probe error message (e.g. 'You do not have permission to access glm-5.1') in model cards instead of the generic 'Service temporarily unavailable' text.
summary.outcome: Fixed the root cause: `localizeRpcError` in `ws-connect.js` was overwriting specific server error messages with generic locale strings before `testModel` could see them. Patched `localizedRpcErrorMessage` in `helpers.js` to preserve the original server message when one is provided, and updated `testModel` in `provider-validation.js` to prefer the raw server message.

repo: All WebSocket RPC error responses are localized in `ws-connect.js` line ~96 via `localizeRpcError` before any caller sees them — this is a global intercept point that can silently swallow specific error messages.
ServiceError::Message (non-Forbidden variants) maps to error code UNAVAILABLE in service-traits/src/lib.rs line ~51, causing the generic locale string to replace all specific backend error text.

There are three separate model card rendering code paths: Settings page (page-providers.js), Onboarding model selector (onboarding-view.js), and Preferred Models modal (providers.js) — each required its own inline error display fix.

code.finding: frame.error = localizeRpcError(frame.error) runs on every inbound WS RPC response, replacing error.message with a locale string before resolving the pending promise.
localizedRpcErrorMessage returns the locale translation keyed on error.code, ignoring error.message entirely — fixed to preserve original message when server provides one.
testModel reads res?.error?.message for probe error display — updated to prefer raw server message over localized fallback.
ServiceError variants other than Forbidden serialize to ErrorShape with code UNAVAILABLE, which triggers the generic locale message in the frontend.

workflow: When frontend shows a generic error but backend logs show a specific one, check for a global WS response interceptor that may be localizing/overwriting error messages.
Tracing the full error path (backend enum → ErrorShape serialization → WS frame → client intercept → caller) was necessary to find the localization clobber.

friction: The error was masked at the WS layer, requiring tracing through ws-connect.js, helpers.js, service-traits, and provider-validation.js before the clobber point was identified.
Initial investigation was misdirected toward cached stale unsupportedReason data and parse_chat_error before the WS intercept was found.
Multiple grep/read cycles were needed because the error transformation happened at a global middleware layer not obvious from the calling code.
open_items: The UNAVAILABLE error code is a broad catch-all for all non-Forbidden ServiceErrors — consider introducing more granular error codes (e.g. PERMISSION_DENIED, RATE_LIMITED) so the frontend can localize meaningfully without discarding specific messages.

The three separate model card code paths (Settings, Onboarding, Preferred Models modal) each needed independent fixes — consolidating them into a shared component would prevent similar divergence in the future.
